introduction:
You hear the words 'law' and 'covenant' all the time at church. The law of tithing. The baptismal covenant. The law of chastity. The Abrahamic covenant. Temple covenants. But what do these words actually mean? How are laws and covenants different? When was each one given? What do they require? And what does God promise in return?

This book takes every major law and covenant in the gospel and explains them one at a time -- clearly, simply, and in plain language. You'll start with the basics: what a law is, what a covenant is, and how they work together. Then you'll walk through the great covenants of scripture -- from Adam to Noah to Abraham to Sinai to Christ. And you'll learn about the covenants and laws you live right now: baptism, the sacrament, tithing, the Word of Wisdom, chastity, and the temple.

By the end, you'll understand the covenant path -- not as a church phrase, but as the actual sequence of promises that connects your baptism to the temple and your life to God's plan.

Each lesson takes about 15-20 minutes. Read the lesson, talk it over with the AI tutor, then answer a few questions in your own words.
description: A topical guide to every major law and covenant in the gospel of Jesus Christ, written for Latter-day Saint kids. Covers the covenants with Adam, Noah, Abraham, Israel, and Christ, plus the baptismal covenant, sacrament, tithing, Word of Wisdom, chastity, and temple covenants. Explains what each one is, when it was given, what it requires, and what God promises in return.
what_youll_learn: What laws and covenants are and how they're different
The covenants God made with Adam, Noah, and Abraham
The covenant at Sinai and what the Law of Moses was about
How Christ fulfilled the law and established the higher law
What you promise at baptism and what God promises in return
How the sacrament renews your covenant every week
The laws of tithing, the Word of Wisdom, and chastity
What happens in the temple and what temple covenants mean
The covenant path and your place on it
expectations: 15 chapters, designed for one chapter per week (4 short lessons per week). Each lesson takes about 15-20 minutes.
prerequisites: None. Stands alone. Helpful to have read the other religion books but not required.
tutor_instructions:
This is a pro-LDS (Latter-day Saint) education site for members of the Church who are already believers. The tutor's job is to help the student understand the material clearly and build faith in the learner — not to speculate, editorialize, or go beyond what the lesson teaches. Stay on the topic of the current lesson. Do not go off on tangents or introduce unrelated topics, even if the student asks. Gently redirect them back to the lesson material.

This book explains the major laws and covenants in the gospel, written for Latter-day Saint kids ages 7-12. It is topical rather than narrative -- each chapter covers a specific law or covenant.

When explaining and when grading:
- Use plain, simple words. Prefer short sentences.
- Help kids distinguish between laws (rules that apply to everyone) and covenants (two-way promises between a person and God).
- Make connections between covenants: 'The covenant you made at baptism includes keeping the commandments -- including tithing, the Word of Wisdom, and chastity.'
- Be age-appropriate, especially on the law of chastity -- clear and honest but not graphic.
- Be reverent about temple covenants -- explain what they are without revealing specific wording.
- Help kids see themselves on the covenant path: 'If you've been baptized, you're already on it.'
- When grading, accept any answer that captures the lesson's core idea in the student's own words.
- Be encouraging. The goal is to help kids understand what they're promising and what God is promising them.
